---
title: "NatureCubeR Package Tutorial"
author: "Adam Varley"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(NatureCubeR)
library(jsonlite)

# Project Data Tutorial

This document describes the core project-data workflow in `NatureCubeR`:
authenticating, browsing your project's sensors and media, and managing
species labels.

## What the workflow does

- Confirms the active project using your API key
- Retrieves sensor/station metadata as a spatial (`sf`) object
- Plots station locations on an interactive map
- Fetches media assets for one or more stations
- Browses and searches species labels (project and IUCN)
- Pushes updated labels back to the platform

---

## Prerequisites

Set your credentials in `~/.Renviron` and restart R:

```
NATURECUBE_API_KEY=your_api_key_here
NATURECUBE_URL=https://naturecube.io/api/
```

See [auth_tutorial.md](auth_tutorial.md) for full authentication details.

---

## Step 1 — Authenticate

```r
library(NatureCubeR)

api_key <- get_key()
hdr <- auth_headers(api_key)
```

---

## Step 2 — Confirm the active project

```r
get_project(hdr = hdr)
```

Prints the project name associated with your API key.

---

## Step 3 — Retrieve station metadata and plot locations

```r
stations <- get_station_info(hdr = hdr, datatype = "image")

plot_stations(stations)
```

`get_station_info()` returns an `sf` object — one row per sensor deployment —
with columns including `device_id`, `record_count`, and deployment timestamps.
`datatype` accepts `"video"`, `"audio"`, `"image"`, or `"eDNA"`.

`plot_stations()` renders an interactive leaflet map with circle markers sized
by `record_count`.

---

## Step 4 — Fetch media assets for a station

```r
psr_ids <- stations$project_system_record_id

media_assets <- get_media_assets(
  hdr      = hdr,
  datatype = "image",
  psrID    = psr_ids
)
```

Pass a vector of `project_system_record_id` values from the stations object.
Returns a tibble with one row per media segment, including `segment_record_id`
and any existing label assignments.

---

## Step 5 — Browse and manage species labels

### 5a — Project labels

```r
project_labels <- get_project_labels(hdr = hdr, labeltype = "Camera")
```

Returns the species labels currently available to labellers on the dashboard.
`labeltype` accepts `"Camera"` or `"Bioacoustic"`.

### 5b — Search the IUCN database

```r
iucn_results <- getIUCNLabels(
  hdr         = hdr,
  limit       = 2000,
  offset      = 0,
  search_term = "Domestic horse"
)
```

Results are in `iucn_results$data`. Pagination metadata (`$total`, `$offset`,
`$limit`) supports iterative calls for large result sets. Omit `search_term`
to return all species.

### 5c — Add new IUCN species to the project

```r
# Prepare a data frame with the required IUCN columns, then upload in chunks.
# example_data <- readLines("path/to/species.json") |> jsonlite::fromJSON()
# example_data$extant_country_list <- NA
# add_IUCN_labels(hdr = hdr, labels = example_data, chunksize = 500)
```

---

## Step 6 — Push updated labels back to the platform

```r
new_label_id <- project_labels[
  project_labels$common_name == "Uneven-toothed Rat",
  "label_id"
]

submission_frame <- data.frame(
  segment_record_id_fk  = media_assets$segment_record_id[1],
  label_id_fk           = new_label_id,
  number_of_individuals = 1
)

push_new_labels(hdr = hdr, submission_records = submission_frame, chunksize = 30)
```

Build one row per media segment you want to label. `chunksize` controls how
many records are sent per API request.

---

## Full tutorial script

See `tutorials/project_file.R` for a complete, runnable example.
